Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Name: Swadloon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Heal 100 damage from each of your Basic Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Deerling
Flavor-Text: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

Name: Sawsbuck
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

Name: Shelmet
Ability-Effect: If you have Karrablast in play, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn or the turn you play it.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use attacks.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

Name: Tepig
Flavor-Text: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

Name: Pignite
Flavor-Text: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

Name: Emboar
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

Name: Simisear
Flavor-Text: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.
Flavor-Text: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

Name: Chandelure
Effects: If you have 10 or more Basic [R] Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

Name: Heatmor
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of [R] Pokémon and Basic [R] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

Name: Reshiram ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Oshawott
Flavor-Text: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

Name: Dewott
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

Name: Samurott
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

Name: Basculin
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food–peat moss.

Name: Swanna
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

Name: Vanillite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

Name: Keldeo ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blitzle
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Joltik
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [F] Energy attached, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

Name: Woobat
Flavor-Text: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

Name: Yamask
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

Name: Cofagrigus
Effects: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

Name: Gothita
Flavor-Text: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

Name: Gothitelle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 3 cards.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Frillish
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

Name: Jellicent ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Roggenrola
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.
Flavor-Text: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

Name: Boldore
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has [F] Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched [F] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

Name: Sawk
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t a Pokémon ex, this attack does nothing. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

Name: Archen
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

Name: Archeops
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may devolve 1 of your opponent’s evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can’t help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Flip a coin for each [D] Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

Name: Zorua
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Vullaby
Flavor-Text: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

Name: Mandibuzz
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less that you find there onto your opponent’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Hydreigon ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

Name: Durant
Effects: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Hyper Fang attack’s base damage is 240.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

Name: Herdier
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

Name: Bouffalant ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Move a Basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Antique Plume Fossil
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Description: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic [C] Pokémon. This card can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Brave Bangle
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn’t have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Cheren
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Harlequin
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Hilda
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Tool Scrapper
Description: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s) and discard them.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ignition Energy
Description: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.If this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon, it provides [C][C][C] Energy instead.

Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

Flavor-Text: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can’t help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: Flip a coin for each [D] Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

Name: Zorua
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Vullaby
Flavor-Text: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

Name: Mandibuzz
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon with 70 HP or less that you find there onto your opponent’s Bench.
Flavor-Text: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

Name: Durant
Effects: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Hyper Fang attack’s base damage is 240.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

Name: Herdier
Effects: Switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

Name: Stoutland
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Reshiram ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Keldeo ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jellicent ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hydreigon ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bouffalant ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Harlequin
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Hilda
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Whimsicott ex
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Reshiram ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Keldeo ex
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jellicent ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t play any Item cards or Pokémon Tool cards from their hand.
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 80 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hydreigon ex
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Bouffalant ex
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hilda
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Victini
Effects: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Reshiram ex
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
